query: Does the Steel type resist Ghost and Dark attacks?
expected_answer: No. Steel lost its Ghost and Dark resistances in Generation 6; it resisted them up to Generation 5.
notes: PRIMARY cold open. Stale Gen-5 chart (still lists Ghost/Dark) is pinned to the top of the skewed duplication tail (8x) and wins retrieval, so the agent answers 'yes' (wrong). Obscure enough that Haiku does NOT leak the answer from memory. Fix: is_current==True filter.

query: Which Pokemon puts its prey to sleep and then eats their dreams?
expected_answer: Drowzee. It puts enemies to sleep and eats their dreams.
notes: semantic paraphrase; weak vs strong recall across corpus sizes

query: A Pokemon that copies an enemy's genetic code to transform into a duplicate of it
expected_answer: Ditto copies an enemy's genetic code to transform into a copy of the enemy.
notes: semantic

query: A Pokemon whose footprints contain poison because it is so toxic
expected_answer: Muk is so toxic that even its footprints contain poison.
notes: semantic; weak model missed this at baseline

query: A Pokemon that wears a skull helmet so no one has seen its real face
expected_answer: Cubone never removes its skull helmet, so no one has seen its face.
notes: semantic

query: A Pokemon made entirely of programming code that can move through cyberspace
expected_answer: Porygon consists entirely of programming code and can move freely in cyberspace.
notes: semantic

query: A Pokemon created by scientists through years of gene-splicing experiments
expected_answer: Mewtwo was created by a scientist through gene splicing and DNA engineering.
notes: semantic

query: A gentle water Pokemon hunted almost to extinction that ferries people across the sea
expected_answer: Lapras was overhunted almost to extinction and can ferry people across water.
notes: semantic

query: A creature whose irregular genetic code mutates when exposed to elemental stones
expected_answer: Eevee has an irregular genetic code and may mutate when exposed to element stones.
notes: semantic

query: A ghostly Pokemon that slips through walls and is said to come from another dimension
expected_answer: Haunter can slip through walls and is said to be from another dimension.
notes: semantic

query: A rare Pokemon said to bring happiness to whoever manages to catch it
expected_answer: Chansey is a rare Pokemon said to bring happiness to those who get it.
notes: semantic

query: A Pokemon whose body always burns with an orange glow so it can hide among flames
expected_answer: Magmar's body burns with an orange glow, letting it hide among flames.
notes: semantic

query: A Pokemon whose head occasionally drops off and continues on as another Pokemon
expected_answer: Exeggutor. Legend says one of its heads can drop off and continue on as an Exeggcute.
notes: pure dense misses it entirely (query says 'another Pokemon', flavor names 'Exeggcute'); sparse recovers the head/drop overlap, rerank puts it first

query: A Pokemon far weaker now than its stronger ancient ancestors
expected_answer: Magikarp. In the distant past it was stronger than the weak descendants that exist today.
notes: dense misses the inverted perspective (weaker-now vs stronger-past); hybrid recovers it

query: A Pokemon that launches its horns in quick volleys when attacked, and whose innards have never been seen
expected_answer: Cloyster launches its horns in volleys when attacked; its innards have never been seen.
notes: dense ranks it low; rerank lifts it to the top

query: Tell me the Pokedex entry for Charizard
expected_answer: Charizard spits fire hot enough to melt boulders and can cause forest fires.
notes: entity query; top-k fills with duplicate/fragmented charizard chunks until dedup

query: Give me the Pokedex description of Gyarados
expected_answer: Gyarados is huge and vicious, capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.
notes: entity query; duplicate-rate high at baseline

query: What does the Pokedex say about Snorlax?
expected_answer: Snorlax is very lazy and does nothing but eat and sleep.
notes: entity query; duplicate-rate high at baseline

query: What does Drowzee eat, and is that Pokemon weak to Bug-type attacks?
expected_answer: Drowzee eats dreams. It is a Psychic type, which is weak to Bug (and Ghost and Dark) attacks.
notes: trace-only bonus beat; agent decomposes into two retrieval spans (what it eats -> its type -> that type's weakness)

query: A Pokemon that blooms in sunlight and constantly moves to find light sources.
expected_answer: Venusaur. Its plant blooms when absorbing solar energy, and it stays on the move to seek sunlight.
notes: mined (Haiku paraphrase) + verified on deduped collection: dense rank 10, hybrid rank 1

query: A Pokemon that crawls up surfaces using adhesive feet on its short legs.
expected_answer: Caterpie. Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that let it tirelessly climb slopes and walls.
notes: mined (Haiku paraphrase) + verified on deduped collection: dense rank >30, hybrid rank 2

query: A Pokemon that sneaks quietly and consumes bird eggs whole.
expected_answer: Ekans. It moves silently and stealthily and eats the eggs of birds such as Pidgey and Spearow whole.
notes: mined (Haiku paraphrase) + verified on deduped collection: dense rank 6, hybrid rank 1

query: A Pokemon that rolls into a spiky ball to defend itself or get away.
expected_answer: Sandslash. It curls up into a spiny ball when threatened and can roll while curled up to attack or escape.
notes: mined (Haiku paraphrase) + verified on deduped collection: dense rank 26, hybrid rank 2

query: A Pokemon that perks up its auditory organs to detect threats and produces toxin based on horn size.
expected_answer: Nidoran male. It stiffens its ears to sense danger; the larger its horns, the more powerful its venom.
notes: mined (Haiku paraphrase) + verified on deduped collection: dense rank 7, hybrid rank 1

query: A Pokemon that lives in dark caves and uses sound waves to navigate and hunt.
expected_answer: Zubat. It forms colonies in perpetually dark places and uses ultrasonic waves to identify and approach targets.
notes: mined (Haiku paraphrase) + verified on deduped collection: dense rank >30, hybrid rank 3

query: A Pokemon that secretes sweet liquid from its mouth to lure creatures toward it.
expected_answer: Gloom. The fluid oozing from its mouth is not drool but a nectar used to attract prey.
notes: mined (Haiku paraphrase) + verified on deduped collection: dense rank 7, hybrid rank 3

query: A Pokemon that moves by tumbling downhill and passes through barriers without stopping.
expected_answer: Graveler. It rolls down slopes to move and rolls over any obstacle without slowing or changing direction.
notes: mined (Haiku paraphrase) + verified on deduped collection: dense rank >30, hybrid rank 1

query: A Pokemon that has multiple heads and stays alert while others rest.
expected_answer: Dodrio. It uses its three brains to execute complex plans; while two heads sleep, one stays awake.
notes: mined (Haiku paraphrase) + verified on deduped collection: dense rank 19, hybrid rank 1

query: A Pokemon that walks sideways using its large claws for stability and combat.
expected_answer: Krabby. Its pincers are powerful weapons and are also used for balance when walking sideways.
notes: mined (Haiku paraphrase) + verified on deduped collection: dense rank >30, hybrid rank 3

query: A Pokemon that throws skeletal weapons like a returning projectile to defeat opponents.
expected_answer: Marowak. The bone it holds is its key weapon; it throws it skillfully like a boomerang to KO targets.
notes: mined (Haiku paraphrase) + verified on deduped collection: dense rank 24, hybrid rank 1

query: A Pokemon that swims forcefully upstream during fall breeding season
expected_answer: Seaking. In the autumn spawning season they swim powerfully up rivers and creeks.
notes: mined (Haiku paraphrase) + verified on deduped collection: dense rank >30, hybrid rank 4

query: A Pokemon that went extinct because its thick armor prevented it from hunting food.
expected_answer: Omastar. This prehistoric Pokemon died out when its heavy shell made it impossible to catch prey.
notes: mined (Haiku paraphrase) + verified on deduped collection: dense rank 7, hybrid rank 5

query: A Pokemon that was brought back to life from an ancient shell discovered on an old seabed.
expected_answer: Kabuto. It was resurrected from a fossil found in what was once the ocean floor eons ago.
notes: mined (Haiku paraphrase) + verified on deduped collection: dense rank 12, hybrid rank 1

query: A Pokemon that sleeps constantly and gains weight from eating without exercise.
expected_answer: Snorlax. It is very lazy, just eats and sleeps, and grows steadily more slothful as its bulk builds.
notes: mined (Haiku paraphrase) + verified on deduped collection: dense rank 10, hybrid rank 3

query: the electric mouse Pokemon that stores electricity in the pouches on its cheeks
expected_answer: Pikachu. It stores electricity in the pouches on its cheeks.
notes: clone crowding: MiniLM top-10 fills with later-gen Pikachu clones (togedemaru, pichu); MiniLM rank None at baseline AND post-dedup, bge rank 1 both. Gold is gen4 flavor (only entry with 'pouches'), so excluded from the decay curve's gen-1 stage.

query: the pink fairy Pokemon that is said to have flown in on a meteor and dances under the full moon
expected_answer: Clefairy. It is said to have ridden to Earth on a meteor and comes out to dance on nights of the full moon.
notes: clone crowding: MiniLM distracted by later-gen fairies (enamorus); MiniLM rank None at baseline and post-dedup, bge rank 1 both. Moon/meteor lore starts in gen2 flavor, so excluded from the decay curve's gen-1 stage.

query: a crab Pokemon whose pincer can pinch with incredible force but often breaks off
expected_answer: Krabby. Its pincers are powerful weapons that sometimes break off in battle but grow back.
notes: clone crowding: MiniLM distracted by later-gen crabs (clauncher, klawf); MiniLM rank None at baseline / 6 post-dedup, bge rank 1 both.
